questions.question: What is the primary purpose of 'Domain Warming'?

options.text: To make the emails load faster
To build a reputation of trust with Inbox Service Providers (ISPs) like Google and Microsoft
To verify that your credit card is active
To rewrite your email content for better clarity

questions.explanation: ISPs monitor the sending patterns of new domains. Warming simulates natural behavior (opens, replies) to prove the domain isn't a spam bot.

questions.question: How long is the recommended 'Slow Ramp' protocol for a brand new domain?

options.text: 24 hours
1 week
2-4 weeks
3 months

questions.explanation: A 4-week period is the practitioner standard for ensuring a domain is mature enough for real outbound campaigns without being flagged as suspicious.

questions.question: What should you do once you start sending real outreach to leads?

options.text: Turn off the warmup tool to save bandwidth
Increase the real outreach to 500 emails per day immediately
Keep the warmup tool running indefinitely at a low volume
Stop all warming and focus on manual replies

questions.explanation: Warmup interactions act as 'insurance' against spam complaints. Keeping a steady heartbeat of positive interactions helps maintain a high domain health score.

questions.question: Which of these metrics is the best indicator that your domain health is in the 'Danger Zone'?

options.text: Inbox Placement rate drops to 60%
Open rate is above 20%
You receive 2 replies in one day
Your domain is 6 months old

questions.explanation: Healthy domains should have 95%+ inbox placement. A drop to 60% indicates that ISPs are filtering your messages into spam or quarantine.

questions.question: What is the 'Safe Zone' for daily per-domain volume for a solo founder?

options.text: 500-1,000 emails/day
30-50 emails/day
1-5 emails/day
As many as your tool allows

questions.explanation: High-precision solo outreach thrives on quality. Sending 30-50 emails per domain keeps you well within safety limits while providing enough volume for consistent conversions.

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'Heartbeat' interactions in your own words. Why is it more effective to have a constant low-volume warmup than a one-time intensive burst?
questions.promptForAI: Evaluate if the founder understands that ISPs reward consistency and diverse interaction (replies, opens) over simple volume. The 'insurance' metaphor is a good sign of understanding.
